page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-48 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the c4d server family. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture and is equipped with 186.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a resource density of approximately 3.88 GB of RAM per vCPU core, establishing a balanced allocation of compute and memory resources.

This instance type does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external network-attached block storage options. Additionally, the instance does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Due to the lack of local NVMe drives and dedicated graphics hardware, it is designed for workloads that rely purely on centralized CPU execution and network-based storage.

The c4d-standard-48 is suitable for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, web serving, and medium-scale database hosting where dedicated CPU allocation is required. Its balanced resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise applications that do not require specialized hardware accelerators or high-speed local scratch disks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-48 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 186.0 GB of system memory. This resource allocation yields a balanced ratio of approximately 3.88 GB of RAM per core, making it suitable for general-purpose and compute-aligned workloads. The instance does not feature bundled local storage or GPU hardware, requiring external network-attached storage for data persistence. With zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses, network configuration must be managed externally. The c4d-standard-48 is designed for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and enterprise workloads that require dedicated CPU resources without the need for specialized hardware acceleration or local NVMe storage. It offers a balanced, qualitative cost-to-resource profile for standard enterprise deployments.

tagline: A dedicated forty-eight core x86_64 virtual machine offering a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
